Day 100 major signals: 5th kinetic cycle (US radar strikes Goruk/Sirik/Qeshm; Iran 7 ballistic missiles Kuwait+Bahrain; 6 intercepted, 1 failed; no US KIA); Lebanon ceasefire collapsed (Hezbollah rejected US-brokered deal; IDF continued strikes; Trump called Netanyahu expletive); MOU at impasse (Trump 'final determination' meeting ended without decision; draft sent back to Iran); MBS strongest condemnation of Iran yet (supports 'any measures' Kuwait/Bahrain take); Putin confirmed SPIEF June 4 (BS-9.3 June sequence not triggered). Covers Days 98-100 (June 4-6).

No new T1/T2 bazaar signal this cycle; BS-1b remains at M confidence provisional. Two consecutive cycles at M confidence (Day 97 + Day 100) without T1/T2 upgrade; recommend daily monitor per PROBE-3 protocol upgrade at next audit.

sources.name: CENTCOM official releases (T1: Iran fired seven missiles toward Kuwait and Bahrain; six intercepted)
ABC News / Al Jazeera / UPI / Iran International / Middle East Eye (5th kinetic cycle)
The National (Kuwait engagement / no casualties)

finding_cards.finding: 5th kinetic cycle materialized June 5-6. US forces shot down four Iranian one-way attack drones fired toward the Strait of Hormuz, then struck Iranian coastal surveillance radar sites at Goruk and Sirik (new sites, not previously struck) and Qeshm Island. Iran retaliated with seven ballistic missiles at Kuwait (four) and Bahrain (three); CENTCOM confirmed six intercepted, one failed to reach its target. Kuwait army confirmed engagement with no reported injuries this cycle; no US casualties; Iranian claim of damaging US 5th Fleet Bahrain headquarters false per CENTCOM. Self-defense framing throughout; no new operation name; Trump deal-direction concurrent — PROBE-7 discriminator classifies as Fork C inadvertent-escalation signal, NOT Fork A resumption.

finding_cards.conflict_notes: No source conflict on 5th kinetic cycle facts; CENTCOM T1 confirmed missile intercept count (6 intercepted, 1 failed); Kuwait military confirmed engagement; Iranian 5th Fleet-damage claim adjudicated false at H (CENTCOM T1 denial; Bahrain neutral-country confirmation of intercepts). Principal validation (Step 7b): Decision to fire 7 ballistic missiles at Kuwait+Bahrain attributed to IRGC command (Vahidi/council). Alternative principal hypothesis: Was this a Vahidi unilateral decision or council-authorized? Discriminating evidence: IRGC framed as coordinated 'self-defense' response (not rogue action); consistent with council-level authorization. No discriminating evidence this cycle to distinguish Vahidi-direct from council-collective authorization. Attribution: 'IRGC command; inherited, not validated at individual level this cycle.'

sources.name: Fortune / Yahoo Finance (Brent $92.87-95 on June 5, -2.27% day)
Trading Economics / CME (Brent backwardation continued)

finding_cards.finding: Brent fell to $92.87-95 on June 5, down from $97.81 at Day 97, despite the 5th kinetic cycle. Market pricing the 5th cycle as ceasefire-edge attrition (consistent with the PROBE-7 discriminator), not a Fork A campaign restart. Partial offset from Iran deal-optimism (Trump holding deal-direction). Chinese crude imports fell to lowest in 10 years, weighing on demand-side pricing. Backwardation still elevated. No Kharg saturation event. No commercial shipping restoration.
finding_cards.conflict_notes: No source conflict. The price decline on a kinetic-escalation day is counter-intuitive but consistent with market reading 5th cycle as self-defense: the PROBE-7 discriminator and the market pricing are aligned. No breakout above $115 (Fork A repricing threshold). Brent at $92-95 is below the $95 Fork B credibility signal threshold — market not yet pricing deal as highly probable.

sources.name: NPR / Axios (Trump-Netanyahu 'f***ing crazy' call; Trump reins in Netanyahu on Lebanon)

finding_cards.finding: Lebanon ceasefire collapsed within 48 hours of the June 3 US-Lebanon-Israel framework text. Israel-Lebanon agreed; Hezbollah rejected the deal outright (leader Naim Qassem: 'a roadmap to annihilate part of the Lebanese people'; demands full IDF withdrawal from southern Lebanon). IDF continued striking Lebanon June 4-5; 20+ killed in Israeli strikes June 5. Trump announced a ceasefire ('they called us'); Netanyahu denied it and ordered continued IDF operations 'as planned'; Defense Minister Katz denied any ceasefire. Trump-Netanyahu heated call: Trump called Netanyahu expletive for ordering Beirut attack that threatened to derail Iran-US negotiations. Iran: Araghchi re-affirmed 'all fronts' condition — no deal without Lebanon ceasefire. Knesset dissolution: proceeding through readings (two more required after June 3 first plenary 106-0; September elections on track).

discounts_applied: Trump 'they called us' claim on Hezbollah: near-zero without corroboration; Hezbollah official statement is authoritative on Hezbollah's position
finding_cards.conflict_notes: Trump-Netanyahu expletive call is T2 multi-outlet (NPR, Axios, CBC, Time); well-sourced at H confidence. Netanyahu's response ('we can disagree and by afternoon have common action') is on-record CNBC. The Lebanon ceasefire collapse is T2 multi-outlet at H. Principal validation (Step 7b): Lebanon clause blocking attributed to Netanyahu (demand for operational freedom) + Hezbollah (outright rejection). Alternative for Netanyahu: IDF chief Zamir (Day 77 baseline said 'all goals achieved'; IDF-coalition asymmetry on Lebanon terms may mean Zamir could accept sustainable ceasefire). Discriminating evidence: Netanyahu explicitly denied the ceasefire and ordered continued operations — this is a named principal-level decision attributable to Netanyahu, not ambiguous Zamir-vs-Netanyahu framing. Attribution this cycle: Netanyahu confirmed as the decision-maker on Lebanon continuation.

sources.name: PolitiFact (Senate WPR status June 4)
NPR / WaPo (House WPR passage June 3 carry-forward)
White House (concurrent resolution unconstitutional; Chadha 1983 precedent)

finding_cards.finding: Senate has not scheduled an on-merits WPR vote as of June 6. Senate procedural advance from prior cycle (50-47 discharge) remains the operative baseline; a final vote has not been scheduled. White House explicitly invoked the 1983 INS v. Chadha precedent to characterize concurrent resolutions as unconstitutional legislative vetoes, adding a new constitutional-layer challenge to the T9 Stage-2 hysteresis architecture. No Federal court WPA challenge filed. T9 disc-ratio holds at 4:10; /premortem flag from Day 97 outstanding.
finding_cards.conflict_notes: White House constitutional framing (Chadha) is legally contested but not yet tested in court. The 1983 Chadha ruling was about legislative vetoes in statutes, not concurrent resolutions per se; legal scholars dispute direct applicability. This is a T9 executive-assertion event that advances the Stage-2 hysteresis architecture (executive building legal case that WPR is unenforceable) rather than a development that challenges it. T9 VALIDATED holds.

finding_cards.finding: Trump's 'final determination' Situation Room meeting ended without a decision (May 29). Trump sent the MOU draft back to Iran demanding two amendments: (a) specific timing and extent of Iran's nuclear commitment performance; (b) stipulation that Iran ends Strait control 'immediately' after MOU signing. Iran countered with a demand for $24 billion in frozen asset release as deal condition. As of June 6, Araghchi: 'no formal negotiation process underway; messages continue.' Lebanon clause: now blocked at Hezbollah level (not just Israeli terms) — Araghchi's 'all fronts' condition cannot be satisfied while Hezbollah rejects ceasefire and IDF continues strikes. LOI architecture: conceptually alive but not signed.

finding_cards.probe_name: Mutual Dispositional Reading and Power-Shift Perception

sources.name: NPR / Axios (Trump-Netanyahu strained call; Lebanon dispute)
CNBC / Times of Israel (Netanyahu 'disagree and have common action'; Israel ready to strike)

finding_cards.finding: Trump-Netanyahu dispositional gap is now at maximum documented intensity (expletive call; Trump reining in Netanyahu while Netanyahu defies on Lebanon). Trump remains deal-leaning; Netanyahu remains maximalist on Lebanon and strike-readiness rhetoric. No new Israeli operational signals (no air-refueling tempo escalation, no F-35/F-15 forward-positioning reports this cycle). Netanyahu routing strike decision to Trump explicitly ('Israel is ready and US forces are ready; decision is up to Trump') — rhetorical spoiler posture, not operational graduation. T8 Powell amplifier: held at maximum loading.
finding_cards.conflict_notes: No source conflict. Netanyahu's 'disagree in morning, common action by afternoon' framing suggests he is not breaking with Trump on the deal track (nuclear dimension); Lebanon is the specific axis of defiance. This distinction matters for Fork B durability: Netanyahu spoiler mechanism on Lebanon is operational; on the nuclear-deal dimension, Netanyahu is still routing decisions to Trump.

sources.name: Cross-probe composite: PROBE-7, PROBE-9, PROBE-12', PROBE-13, PROBE-14

finding_cards.finding: Multiple first-mover thresholds advancing simultaneously this cycle: (1) Iranian-side: BS-1b second consecutive cycle at M confidence; oil-revenue structural constraint confirmed; no T1/T2 escalation yet; (2) Trump A1: final-determination meeting without decision; draft sent back; deal-direction strained but holding — 3+ days post-Lebanon-collapse without abandonment; (3) Israeli threshold: Lebanon ceasefire collapsed; Netanyahu defying Trump on Lebanon; Knesset dissolution advancing; Powell at maximum — but NO operational graduation (no air-refueling tempo, no forward-positioning); rhetoric vs operational gap persists; (4) WPR: Senate not scheduling; symbolically constraining Fork A; raising Israeli pre-emption relative value; (5) Strangulation: Brent $92-95 (price declining not rising despite kinetic cycle); no Kharg event; no product-scarcity onset. Joint distribution: Iranian-first-mover and Israeli-pre-emption thresholds both advancing without resolution; Trump-A1 strained but held; accident-surface expansion (5th kinetic cycle) is the dominant short-term Fork C driver.

finding_cards.conflict_notes: PROBE-16 is a synthetic composite; it consumes PROBE-7/9/12'/13/14 outputs. The joint pattern is more alarming than any individual probe: the accident surface (5th kinetic cycle), the diplomatic surface (Lebanon collapse), and the principal surface (MOU impasse) are all simultaneously under stress. Yet no single threshold has been breached at the operational level: no US KIA, no IDF air-refueling tempo, no Iranian decision-maker named defection. The framework is in a compressed holding pattern: multiple threshold approaches without resolution.

sources.name: NPR / APT YouTube (Putin SPIEF St. Petersburg appearance June 4)
en.kremlin.ru events calendar (June 4 confirmed appearance)

finding_cards.finding: Putin confirmed at the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum (SPIEF) June 4, answering questions from international journalists and news agencies. He discussed Ukraine/Trump Anchorage summit commitments and Russia's air-defense posture. June public-appearance count: at least 1 (SPIEF June 4). BS-9.3 threshold assessment: 3 consecutive months <2 appearances/month requires April + May + June all below 2. May count: 1-2 (Lukashenko May 8; Victory Parade May 9). April count: unresolved. June is at minimum 1 — unless further confirmed appearances bring June to 2+, June does NOT break the threshold sequence, but a June count of exactly 1 means the 3-month sequence (if April <2 and May <2) is still possible. BS-9.3 NOT fired as of June 6.

trigger_detail.appendix_b_row: BS-9.3 Putin public appearance frequency threshold
trigger_detail.direction: no change — SPIEF confirmed appearance; threshold not triggered for June
trigger_detail.gap_to_full_match: April appearance count is the binding unresolved variable; if April confirmed <2 AND May <2 AND June ends <2 (currently at 1), BS-9.3 would fire at the end of June

finding_cards.conflict_notes: Putin's SPIEF appearance is confirmed at T1 (Kremlin calendar) and T2 (NPR, YouTube). The appearance was substantive (press conference, policy statements on Ukraine/Trump summit). This is not a pro forma appearance; Putin appeared cognitively and physically active (based on described content). BS-9.3 monitoring priority: the April count remains the binding analytical gap. If April is confirmed at <2 (last available data from prior sweeps was 'April 24 + April 27 referenced'), then April = 2, which does NOT meet the <2 threshold — April 2 appearances would mean BS-9.3 cannot fire for the April-May-June sequence regardless of May-June counts. Recommend retrieval of April Kremlin calendar data as the definitive BS-9.3 resolution.

finding_cards.probe_name: Gulf State Troika and Unaligned-Middle Pivot Capacity

sources.name: Al Arabiya English (Saudi FM condemnation of Iranian attacks on Kuwait and Bahrain)
The National / Iran International (MBZ + MBS joint warning; Gargash 'cohesive Gulf stance')
Jerusalem Post / Al Jazeera (Gulf state responses to 5th kinetic cycle)

finding_cards.finding: Saudi FM issued formal condemnation of Iranian attacks on Kuwait and Bahrain, citing sovereignty violation and regional security threat. MBS affirmed 'full solidarity' with Kuwait and Bahrain and stated the Kingdom is ready to support 'any measures' they take in response. MBZ and MBS issued a joint warning that Iranian attacks on GCC members risk regional escalation. UAE presidential adviser Gargash called for 'firm, unified, cohesive Gulf stance.' This is the strongest Saudi public condemnation of Iranian attacks since the conflict began — MBS moved from quiet accommodation/backchannel to explicit public solidarity with attacked GCC members. However: no explicit Saudi or UAE support for US military offensive action against Iran; condemnation is directed at Iran's GCC-targeting, not an endorsement of US Fork A resumption. BS-18 brake mechanism: strained but not fractured.

trigger_detail.appendix_b_row: BS-18 MBS accommodation pathway strain — 'full solidarity' and 'any measures' language exceeds prior backchannel-only posture
trigger_detail.framework_variable: BS-18 accommodation pathway durability; Gulf troika brake fracture threshold
trigger_detail.direction: decay signal — single-cycle; MBS public condemnation of Iran attacks on GCC members strains the accommodation logic without fracturing the brake
trigger_detail.gap_to_full_match: Full fracture would require explicit Saudi/UAE support for US offensive military action OR MBS/MBZ public withdrawal from brake posture; this cycle: condemnation only, not fracture

variable_moved.prior_value: Strained (Abraham Accords demand context; calendar-structural silence)
variable_moved.new_value: Further strained — MBS now publicly supporting 'any measures' Kuwait/Bahrain take against Iran; Saudi-Iran accommodation logic under active test
variable_moved.driver: Iran fired 7 ballistic missiles at Kuwait and Bahrain; Saudi publicly condemns; MBS solidarity statement crosses prior accommodation-only posture

discounts_applied: Al Arabiya English: Saudi state-adjacent media; read against the grain on Saudi posture toward Iran — directionally reliable; discount not applied on condemnation statement
MBZ-MBS 'joint warning' described by The National (UAE state-adjacent): credible on UAE-Saudi coordination; no discount
finding_cards.conflict_notes: Critical distinction: MBS supporting 'any measures Kuwait/Bahrain take' is NOT equivalent to MBS supporting US military offensive action against Iran. Kuwait's measures (expelling Iranian diplomats, summoning ambassador) are diplomatic, not military. The language is consistent with MBS defending the GCC-solidarity norm without endorsing US Force resumption. This is a single-cycle decay signal that should be flagged at next /audit for BS-18 MBS-accommodation-pathway risk reassessment, but NOT a brake-fracture trigger. Alternative reading: MBS may be signaling to Iran that GCC solidarity has limits — a coercive communication through condemnation to preserve the accommodation logic by demonstrating its limits. Discriminating evidence between these readings: whether Saudi Arabia separately maintains any backchannel to Iran post-Kuwait attack (June 22 Lebanon talks window is the next discriminating event).

finding_cards.finding: P-AIM: Iranian official demand-set ($24B frozen assets + Lebanon all-fronts + immediate Hormuz sovereignty) remains bounded in limited-aims range — no order-revisionist escalation (expulsion of US from Gulf, elimination of Israel) confirmed. P-INFO: Vahidi blocking Pezeshkian appointments continues from Day 97; IRGC information monopoly advancing — P-INFO signal active at T3 confidence. P-DG: No US targeting of named Iranian principals this cycle (radar sites struck, not named individuals). P-DG2: No post-strike demand hardening confirmed at T2 — Iran's $24B demand preceded the 5th cycle; no documented escalation of terms post-Goruk/Sirik/Qeshm strikes. P-CHEMO: US strikes remain on radar/air-defense infrastructure (maritime-counter mode); no ground-force buildup signals. P-OVEX: IRGC capability 'increased' during ceasefire — proxy network not showing self-amputation; reverse.
discounts_applied: Iran $24B frozen-asset demand: CNN T2 unnamed Iranian official; -30% confidence on specific figure; direction (Iran has financial demands) well-corroborated
finding_cards.conflict_notes: P-INFO is the most active Paine sub-signal this cycle: the IRGC structural blockade of Pezeshkian (blocking intelligence appointments, governance-axis discrimination via Vahidi statements) confirms L5 PA-gap actively manufactured rather than merely structural. This aligns with Paine's qualitative prediction that survival-driven information monopoly degrades apex decision quality. No P-DG2 escalation detected; P-AIM remains in limited-aims territory. Framework implication: Paine architecture remains in the background-monitoring state.

trigger_digest.appendix_b_row: CENTCOM self-defense strikes inside Iran (5th kinetic cycle); Fork C inadvertent-escalation signal

proposed_revision.edit: Fork C 28-36% → 30-39%. 5th kinetic cycle: US struck radar sites at Goruk, Sirik, and Qeshm; Iran fired 7 ballistic missiles at Kuwait (4) + Bahrain (3); 6 intercepted, 1 failed; no US KIA. Accident surface now includes ballistic-missile-range targeting of multiple GCC members simultaneously. PROBE-7 discriminator: self-defense framing, reactive, no new operation name, deal-direction non-reversing → Fork C nudge not Fork A. T12 advance through use (5th cycle). Trend cross-check: T2 ADVANCE (Iran fires and denies civilian targeting; Mosaic-Octopus calibration); T12 ADVANCE.

trigger_digest.appendix_b_row: Lebanon clause definitively blocking — diplomatic-spoiler active at two independent levels

proposed_revision.section: BS-11 LOI architecture; Fork D' probability; Lebanon clause status
proposed_revision.edit: Lebanon clause status: COLLAPSED. From 'conditional framework text; partially bridged' (Day 97) to 'Lebanon ceasefire collapsed — Hezbollah rejection + IDF non-compliance; Lebanon clause now blocked at two independent levels.' Hezbollah leader Qassem explicitly rejected the US-brokered Israel-Lebanon ceasefire deal as 'a roadmap to annihilate part of the Lebanese people' and pledged continued attacks. IDF continued strikes killing 20+ June 5 despite announced ceasefire. Iran's 'all fronts' condition cannot be satisfied while Hezbollah refuses ceasefire and IDF continues. Lebanon clause is now a tripartite blocking obstacle: Israel-Lebanon-Hezbollah incoherence + Iran's insistence on all-fronts condition + US-Iran dispute over whether Lebanon was ever included in the ceasefire. Fork D' (30d): downward pressure; held 22-30% but lower-bound under active threat. June 22 Lebanon talks remain scheduled but Hezbollah rejection makes the timeline optimistic. Trend cross-check: T8 ADVANCE (Powell spoiler mechanism confirmed operational; Netanyahu defying Trump on Lebanon = spoiler strategy in execution); T4 ADVANCE (Trump expletive call = deal-faction at maximum resistance to maximalist).

trigger_digest.appendix_b_row: Trump-Netanyahu principal-level conflict at maximum intensity; Penetration mechanism active-but-contested

proposed_revision.section: BS-12 US-side PA-gap; A1 oscillation state; T4 eschatological-coalition disaggregation
proposed_revision.edit: Trump-Netanyahu Penetration mechanism state updated: ACTIVE-but-CONTESTED at maximum documented intensity. Trump called Netanyahu expletive on heated call (T2 multi-outlet: NPR, Axios, CBC); Trump explicitly reined in Netanyahu over Lebanon Beirut operations. This is the strongest documented Trump principal-level resistance to Netanyahu maximalism in the conflict's window — exceeding the Day 84 'tense hour-long call.' A1 oscillation: Trump held deal-direction for 3+ days post-Lebanon-collapse without abandonment (sent MOU draft back with amendments rather than withdrawing). T4 advance: deal-faction (Trump) overriding maximalist (Netanyahu) at maximum friction, 10th-plus cycle without §5.20 counter-mobilization. Netanyahu is defying Trump on Lebanon specifically while still routing Iran-strike decision to Trump — suggests Lebanon = spoiler tactic; nuclear-deal = separate track. Fork B durability: strained but not collapsed (Trump still in deal-direction).

trigger_digest.appendix_b_row: MOU impasse — Trump final-determination meeting without decision; draft sent back; LOI architecture stalled

proposed_revision.section: BS-11 MOU architecture; Fork D' LOI architecture status
proposed_revision.edit: LOI/MOU status: impasse confirmed at T2. Trump Situation Room 'final determination' meeting ended without decision (May 29; T2 CNBC/CBS multi-outlet). Draft sent back to Iran with two amendments: (a) timing/extent of nuclear commitment performance; (b) immediate Hormuz end-of-control after signing. Iran: $24B frozen asset release demanded as condition (CNN T2 unnamed Iranian official; -30% discount on figure; direction corroborated). Araghchi June 6: 'no formal negotiation process underway; messages continue.' LOI architecture conceptually alive but not signed. Lebanon clause now double-blocked (Hezbollah + IDF) compounding the impasse. Note for SITREP: per discount rule, Trump's simultaneous optimism statements ('MOU within a week'; 'deal largely negotiated') carry near-zero weight without tape action; the operative tape this cycle is impasse-confirming.

trigger_digest.appendix_b_row: BS-18 MBS accommodation pathway — 'any measures' language exceeds prior posture; single-cycle decay signal

proposed_revision.section: BS-18 MBS accommodation pathway risk; Gulf troika brake fracture threshold
proposed_revision.edit: BS-18 MBS pathway: single-cycle decay signal. MBS issued 'full solidarity' with Kuwait and Bahrain and stated readiness to support 'any measures they take' in response to Iranian attacks (Al Arabiya English T2; Saudi official statement). MBZ + MBS joint condemnation; Gargash called for 'firm, unified, cohesive Gulf stance.' This is stronger public condemnation language than any prior cycle — MBS moved from quiet backchannel-accommodation to explicit public GCC-solidarity assertion. CRITICAL DISTINCTION: no explicit support for US offensive military action against Iran; Kuwait's 'measures' are diplomatic (expelling Iranian diplomats), not military. This is NOT a brake-fracture trigger per the Appendix B criteria (which requires explicit support for US military action or troika public withdrawal from brake posture). Flag for next /audit: BS-18 MBS accommodation pathway risk should be re-assessed with consideration of whether the Saudi-Iran backchannel remains operational post-Kuwait attacks and whether MBS's 'any measures' language is coercive communication to Iran or genuine fracture signal.
